Q10 Not checked
Technical • MECHANICAL ENGINEERING • +1 / -0
Not attempted

A rod of 4 cm diameter and 25 cm length is maintained at 120°C at one end and 20°C at the other end. These temperature conditions are attained when there is heat flow rate of 8 watts along the length of rod. If the outer cylindrical surface of the rod is completely insulated, the thermal conductivity of the rod material is _____ W/mK. (Round off to two decimal places)

NAT
Correct answer: 15.92 (15.85 - 16.02)
Solution

15.92 (15.85 - 16.02)

decimal places)

NAT
Correct answer: 0.694 (0.690 - 0.698)
Solution

0.694 (0.690 - 0.698)

Technical • MECHANICAL ENGINEERING • +1 / -0

Two long rods of the same diameter, one made of brass (k = 90 W/mK) and the other of copper (k = 380 W/mK), have one of their ends inserted into a furnace. At a section 12.5 cm away from the furnace, the temperature of the brass rod is 140°C. Considering both rods are exposed to the same environment, the distance from the furnace end, the same temperature would be reached in the copper rod is ______ cm. (Round off to two decimal places)

NAT
Correct answer: 25.69 (25.65 - 25.75)
Solution

25.69 (25.65 - 25.75)

Solution

Q15 Not checked
Technical • MECHANICAL ENGINEERING • +1 / -0
Not attempted

Which of the above statement(s) is/are CORRECT regarding a black body?

Correct answer: N/A
Solution

(a, b, c)
The reflectivity and transmissivity of a black body is zero.

Q16 Not checked
Technical • MECHANICAL ENGINEERING • +1 / -0
Not attempted

Which of the above statement(s) is/are INCORRECT regarding emissivity?

Correct answer: N/A
Solution

(a, d)
• The emissivity of metallic surfaces is generally small, achieving values as low as 0.02 for highly polished gold and sliver.
• The emissivity of conductor increases with increasing temperature.

Technical • MECHANICAL ENGINEERING • +2 / -0.66
Not attempted

Two very long pieces of bare copper wire of 1.6 mm in diameter is required to solder together with a solder that melts at 195°C. Considering the thermal conductivity of the wire alloy as 340 W/mK, the minimum energy input required in soldering operation is ________ W. (Correct upto two decimal places)

NAT
Correct answer: 2.68 (2.60 - 2.76)
Solution

2.68 (2.60 - 2.76)

Technical • MECHANICAL ENGINEERING • +2 / -0

A 25 mm thick potato slab (thermal conductivity = 1 W/mK) is roasted with help of microwave heating. If the heat transfer coefficient on the surface of the potato slab is 20 W/m2K, the required heating capacity in W/m3 of the microwave is _____. (Answer in integer)

NAT
Correct answer: 128000 (127950 - 128050)
Solution

128000 (127950 - 128050)

Technical • MECHANICAL ENGINEERING • +2 / -0

A surface at 500 K convects and radiates heat to the surroundings at 350 K. NAT
Correct answer: 1202 (1150 - 1250)
Solution

1202 (1150 - 1250)

(Round off to the nearest integer value)

Correct answer: 1407 (1390 - 1420)
Solution

1407 (1390 - 1420)

Solution
